trustBand.label: Seattle Drainage Planning
trustBand.sub: Every pour graded away from the structure

hero.imageAlt: A freshly poured exposed-aggregate driveway leading to a craftsman home, precise control joints visible, concrete surface perfectly textured
hero.headline: Poured Right. Stays Right.

intro.label: A concrete studio that starts with the base
intro.body: A concrete flatwork and decorative concrete studio serving Bainbridge Island and the north Seattle neighborhoods. Proper base preparation, reinforced pours, and Seattle drainage planning — done right the first time so it doesn't crack, heave, or drain toward your foundation.

personas.label: Driveways & Patios
personas.kicker: For the surface that has to last

personas.label: Foundations & Footings
personas.kicker: For the structure built to code

personas.label: Stamped & Decorative
personas.kicker: For the finish that looks intentional

configuratorTeaser.headline: Get a concrete estimate in under a minute.
configuratorTeaser.body: Tell us the project type and square footage for a transparent price range before any site visit.

zPatternRows.label: The Base
zPatternRows.headline: A concrete job is won or lost before the truck arrives.
zPatternRows.body: Demo, excavation, and sub-base preparation are where most driveways fail — not the mix or the pour. We compact the base in lifts, set drainage grade away from the structure, and place reinforcement to spec before the first yard of concrete is ordered.

zPatternRows.label: Seattle Drainage
zPatternRows.headline: Poured flat is the wrong grade for Seattle.
zPatternRows.body: Every surface needs to shed water away from your foundation and toward your drainage points. We set grade before we pour — not after. A patio that drains toward the house isn't a patio, it's a foundation problem.

zPatternRows.label: Control Joints
zPatternRows.headline: Concrete cracks. Control joints decide where.
zPatternRows.body: All concrete will eventually crack. Properly placed control joints guide cracks to the right place — the joint — instead of randomly across the surface. We place them at correct spacing for your slab dimensions, not wherever is convenient.

zPatternRows.label: The Cure
zPatternRows.headline: We don't walk away on pour day.
zPatternRows.body: Curing compound applied immediately after finishing. We monitor the slab through the cure window and return for final joint sealing. A rushed cure or ignored sealing schedule is how a beautiful pour turns into a surface problem inside two winters.

footerCta.headline: Ready for concrete that stays right through a Seattle winter?
footerCta.subhead: We'll be in touch within a few hours to schedule a site visit.
